story.iWant: a comprehensive format compatibility matrix showing which parameters convert between formats
story.soThat: I can understand conversion limitations and set appropriate expectations before converting my presets
story.tasks: - Create format compatibility matrix document (docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md) with 30+ parameters - Link matrix from landing page (web/index.html) in Supported Formats section - Add quick reference summary table (optional) to landing page - Update README.md with Format Compatibility section and matrix link - Validate matrix accuracy against Epic 1 parameter mapping specs - Create/update CSS for matrix link button styling - Deploy and verify matrix accessibility

criterion.description: Matrix shows parameter support across all 3 formats
criterion.validation: - All 3 formats as columns: NP3, XMP, lrtemplate - Parameters as rows grouped by category (Basic, Tone Curve, Color, Detail, Effects) - At least 30+ parameters documented - Visual indicators: ✅ (supported), ⚠️ (approximated), ❌ (not supported) - Clear headers and legend explaining symbols

- Category grouping: Basic Adjustments, Tone Curve, Color, HSL, Detail, Effects, Calibration - Category headers bold or visually distinct - Readable formatting with adequate column width - User can find "Does X parameter convert?" in <30 seconds

- Consistent symbol (⚠️ or ~) for approximations - Footnote or mapping notes column explaining how approximation works - Approximation strategy section explaining approach - Example: "Highlights → Approximated via Contrast adjustment"

- All unmappable parameters listed (Grain, Vignette, Split Toning, Tone Curves to NP3) - Clear ❌ symbol distinguishable from approximation - Explanation of why not supported (format limitation) - Known Limitations section listing NP3 constraints

artifact.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Format compatibility matrix showing parameter mapping across NP3/XMP/lrtemplate. Clear visual format compatibility table (scannable, includes approximations). All 3 formats documented with parameter categories organized logically.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: 95%+ accuracy goal for color science translation. Bidirectional conversion with high fidelity. Clear documentation of approximations when features don't have 1:1 equivalents.

artifact.title: Parameter Mapping Rules
artifact.snippet: Comprehensive parameter mapping rules covering direct mappings (XMP ↔ lrtemplate), approximation mappings (NP3 ↔ XMP/lrtemplate), and unmappable parameters. Includes formulas: Sharpness ×10, Contrast ×33, Saturation ×33. Documents 50+ parameters with ranges, rationale, and code examples.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Direct mapping for XMP ↔ lrtemplate (40+ parameters 1:1). Approximation mapping for NP3: Sharpening 0-9, Contrast ±3, Saturation ±3. Hub-and-spoke architecture via UniversalRecipe intermediate representation.

artifact.title: Story 1-8: Parameter Mapping Rules (Completed)
artifact.snippet: Validated all formulas against parser/generator code. XMP and lrtemplate use identical parameter names - only syntax differs. NP3 sharpening uses scale factor ×10. HSL adjustments (24 parameters) most critical unmappable category. Round-trip accuracy: 100% XMP ↔ lrtemplate, 95-100% NP3, 60-80% XMP → NP3 → XMP.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Hub-and-spoke conversion design. UniversalRecipe serves as central hub representing superset of all format capabilities. All parameters listed in matrix are fields in UniversalRecipe struct. Direct 1:1 mappings preserve field values exactly.

artifact.title: Story 7-1: Landing Page (Drafted)
artifact.snippet: Landing page established with privacy promise and 3-step usage guide. Story 7-2 provides technical depth for users evaluating Recipe. Progressive disclosure pattern: simple landing page → detailed matrix.

artifact.reason: Central hub struct defining all 50+ parameters with ranges. Reference for matrix parameter list validation. Includes Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, HSL adjustments, and Metadata map.
NP3 → UniversalRecipe mapping formulas: sharpening ×10, contrast ×33, saturation ×33. Reference for approximation strategy accuracy.
UniversalRecipe → NP3 mapping with clamping. Shows range limitations and reverse formulas for matrix documentation.
XMP parameter extraction. Shows all supported XMP fields for matrix column population.
lrtemplate parameter extraction. Confirms identical field names with XMP (1:1 mapping).
Landing page where matrix link will be added. Check existing Supported Formats section structure for integration point.
Repository overview where Format Compatibility section will be added.

ecosystem.name: Go
ecosystem.note: Go toolchain for WASM compilation (Epic 2) and CLI builds (Epic 3). Matrix is static documentation only.

ecosystem.name: Web
ecosystem.note: Static site deployed to Cloudflare Pages. Matrix document linked from landing page.

ecosystem.name: Testing
ecosystem.note: 1,501 total sample files used for Epic 1 validation. Accuracy claims in matrix based on this test suite.

story-context.constraints: - Matrix must accurately reflect Epic 1 implementation (parameter mapping code in internal/formats/) - All parameter names must match UniversalRecipe struct fields - Approximation formulas must match actual code (sharpness ×10, contrast ×33, saturation ×33) - Visual indicators (✅, ⚠️, ❌) must be consistent throughout matrix - Markdown format for docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md (version controlled, easy to maintain) - Landing page integration must preserve existing design from Story 7-1 - Mobile-responsive table formatting (no horizontal scroll on tablets/phones) - No tracking or analytics (privacy-first principle from Epic 2) - English-only documentation for MVP (internationalization deferred) - Conservative legal stance: document reverse-engineered format honestly in Known Limitations

interface.name: Landing Page Link Integration
interface.notes: Link added to Supported Formats section (#formats). Uses .btn-secondary style consistent with Epic 2 design.

interface.name: README.md Link Integration
interface.notes: Link added to new Format Compatibility section in README for developer-facing entry point.

interface.name: Parameter Mapping Reference
interface.signature: Matrix references docs/parameter-mapping.md for detailed formulas
interface.notes: Matrix provides summary table, parameter-mapping.md provides exhaustive technical details and code examples.

tests.standards:
Epic 7 testing focuses on documentation quality and infrastructure validation. No unit tests for this story - testing is manual review and content validation.
**Documentation Review Method:** - Manual peer review for technical accuracy - Non-technical user comprehension test (can user understand limitations in <2 minutes?) - Cross-reference with Epic 1 code to verify mapping accuracy - Link validation (all links functional, correct destinations) - Mobile responsive testing (table readable on phone/tablet)
**Content Validation:** - Compare matrix parameters with internal/models/recipe.go (UniversalRecipe struct) - Verify approximation strategies match Story 1-8 implementation - Confirm unmappable features list complete (Grain, Vignette, Tone Curves to NP3) - Validate accuracy claims (95%+ for core adjustments) against Epic 1 retrospective
**Tools:** - Browser (manual testing) - Grammarly (spelling/grammar check) - Lighthouse (accessibility audit - inherited from Epic 2)
tests.locations: - docs/stories/7-2-format-compatibility-matrix.md (story file with manual test checklist) - docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md (deliverable documentation) - No automated tests - this is static documentation only

ideas.idea: **Test: Verify All 3 Formats in Matrix** - Open docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md - Verify table has columns: NP3, XMP, lrtemplate - Count parameters: must be ≥30 (Basic: 7, Color: 4, HSL: 24, Detail: 5, etc.) - Check legend exists explaining ✅, ⚠️, ❌ symbols - Pass criteria: All 3 formats visible, 30+ parameters, legend present

**Test: Scan Time for Parameter Lookup** - User task: "Does Highlights parameter convert to NP3?" - Start timer, scan matrix - Expected: User finds answer (❌ or ⚠️) in <30 seconds - Verify categories clear: Basic Adjustments, Color, HSL, Detail, Effects, etc. - Pass criteria: <30 second lookup, categories obvious

**Test: Approximation Documentation Quality** - Find all parameters marked with ⚠️ symbol - Verify each has mapping notes explaining approximation - Check Approximation Strategy section exists - Example: "Highlights → Approximated via Contrast adjustment" documented - Pass criteria: All approximations have explanation, strategy section present

**Test: Unmappable Features Complete** - List all parameters marked ❌ for NP3 column - Verify includes: Grain, Vignette, Split Toning, Tone Curves, Clarity, Texture, Dehaze - Check Known Limitations section explains NP3 constraints - Verify distinguishable from approximations (different symbol) - Pass criteria: All unmappable features listed with explanation

**Test: Cross-Reference with Epic 1 Code** - Open internal/models/recipe.go (UniversalRecipe struct) - Compare struct fields with matrix parameters - Verify all matrix parameters exist in struct - Check approximation formulas match internal/formats/np3/generate.go (×10, ×33) - Pass criteria: 100% alignment between matrix and implementation

**Test: Link Validation** - Open web/index.html in browser - Click "View Detailed Compatibility Matrix" link - Verify opens docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md - Repeat for README.md link - Test mobile view (tablet/phone): verify table readable, no horizontal scroll - Pass criteria: All links work, mobile-responsive
